prerequisites: A running Elasticsearch cluster (self-managed or Elastic Cloud) reachable from the connector.
The HTTP(S) URL of the Elasticsearch endpoint to connect to (the `endpoint` config field).
Elasticsearch security enabled so credentials can be issued and validated.
A user or API key with the `manage_own_api_key` cluster privilege if you intend to create an API key, plus read access to the indices you want to extract.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with an Elasticsearch API key. Create one via `POST /_security/api_key` (requires the `manage_own_api_key` cluster privilege) and supply the encoded value, which Elasticsearch sends as the `Authorization: ApiKey <encoded>` header. Maps to the secret field `authenticationMethod.apiKeySecret`.

authMethods.name: Basic authentication (username and password)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with an Elasticsearch user's username and password. Elasticsearch base64-encodes `username:password` and sends it as the `Authorization: Basic <token>` header. The password maps to the secret field `authenticationMethod.password`.

setupSteps.title: Enable security and obtain your endpoint
setupSteps.body: Ensure Elasticsearch security is enabled on your cluster (self-managed or Elastic Cloud). Note the cluster's HTTP(S) URL to use for the required `endpoint` config field.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication method
setupSteps.body: Set the `authenticationMethod` config field to select how the connector authenticates: an API key (`authenticationMethod.apiKeySecret`) or basic auth with a username and password (`authenticationMethod.password`).

setupSteps.title: Create an API key (API key auth)
setupSteps.body: Create an API key with `POST /_security/api_key`, which requires the `manage_own_api_key` cluster privilege. Use the `encoded` value from the response (the base64 encoding of `id:api_key`) as the `authenticationMethod.apiKeySecret` secret.

setupSteps.title: Or provide username and password (basic auth)
setupSteps.body: Alternatively, use an existing Elasticsearch user's credentials. Enter the username and supply the password as the `authenticationMethod.password` secret. Grant the user read access to the indices you want to extract.

sources.title: Create an API key | Elasticsearch API documentation
